items.q: Why is atmospheric nitrogen unavailable to most plants directly?
items.correct: N2 has a strong triple bond that most organisms cannot break.
items.misconception: Because plants cannot reach high enough to access it.
items.why: Students miss the chemistry. Despite 78% of air being N2, the molecule's triple bond is energetically costly to break. Only specialised nitrogen-fixing bacteria (and lightning, industrial Haber process) can convert it to usable forms.

items.q: What is the leading driver of global deforestation?
items.correct: Agriculture (especially cattle ranching, soy, palm oil).
items.why: Students think trees are felled for wood. While logging contributes, the largest driver is land conversion for agriculture - food production drives most tropical forest loss, especially commodities like beef and palm oil.

items.q: Why are top predators most affected by persistent pollutants like mercury?
items.correct: Biomagnification concentrates pollutants at each trophic level up the food chain.
items.misconception: Predators are exposed to more pollution directly.
items.why: Students focus on direct exposure. The mechanism is concentration through eating contaminated prey - small accumulations in lower levels multiply upward, so apex predators have the highest body burdens.

items.q: Norman Borlaug, father of the Green Revolution, won the Nobel Peace Prize in:
items.why: Correct answer: 1970. “1962” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Soil layer containing most organic matter:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: O horizon. “B horizon” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year Borlaug received Nobel Peace Prize?
items.why: The real figure is 1970 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
